title: stephegyne africana
intro: Stephegyne africana, commonly known as African stephegyne, is a plant species in the family Lamiaceae. It is used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The therapeutic actions of Stephegyne africana include wound healing, pain relief, and the management of various infections. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
side_effects: Stephegyne africana can have side effects if used improperly, such as allergic reactions, skin irritation, and respiratory issues. Ingesting the plant can cause nausea, vomiting, and stomach cramps, while excessive exposure may lead to headaches, dizziness, and fatigue. Improper use of Stephegyne africana can also exacerbate pre-existing conditions like hypertension, anxiety, and insomnia. In severe cases, it may trigger anaphylaxis, a life-threatening allergic reaction that requires immediate medical attention.
